dbSchema.title: Database Schema
dbSchema.subtitle: Complete documentation of the haex-vault database schema, including local SQLite tables and sync server schema.

tables.permissions: Extension permissions granted by the user
tables.identities: Local DID identities used to sign requests
tables.spaces: Shared spaces this vault participates in

crdtTables.configs: Per-device HLC node ID and persisted clock state
crdtTables.dirtyTables: Tables with pending local changes to sync
crdtTables.deletedRows: Per-row delete log used to replicate deletions

syncBackends.homeServerUrl: Base URL of the haex-sync-server backend
syncBackends.spaceId: Shared space identifier on the server
syncBackends.lastPushHlc: HLC of the last successful push
syncBackends.lastPullServerAt: Server-side timestamp of the last successful pull

dirtyTables.comment: Tracks which tables have unsynced changes

vaultKeys.encryptedKey: AES-256-GCM ciphertext, only decryptable on the client
vaultKeys.encryptedName: AES-256-GCM ciphertext of the vault name

syncChanges.rowPks: JSON object with primary key values
syncChanges.autoCreated: Used by the pull endpoint as a forward log

overview.title: Schema Overview
overview.intro: haex-vault uses a dual-database architecture: a local encrypted SQLite database on each device, and the haex-sync-server with its own database for cross-device replication.

local.title: Local Database
local.description: SQLite with SQLCipher encryption, storing all user data and extension tables with CRDT columns.

server.title: Sync Server
server.description: haex-sync-server: HTTP + WebSocket service that stores encrypted column-level changes. Authentication is DID-based; the server never sees plaintext data.

crdtColumns.title: CRDT Columns
crdtColumns.intro: Every table that participates in sync has two special CRDT columns added automatically. Deletes are tracked separately via the haex_deleted_rows table - no tombstone column is added to your tables.
crdtColumns.comment: Added to all synced tables automatically
crdtColumns.hlc: Row-level Hybrid Logical Clock (max over all column HLCs)
crdtColumns.columnHlcs: JSON object with HLC per column for column-level merging
crdtColumns.deletesNote: Deletes do NOT add a tombstone column. A BEFORE DELETE trigger logs deletions to haex_deleted_rows.

coreTables.title: Core Tables
coreTables.intro: These tables are part of haex-vault core and exist in every vault. They all participate in CRDT sync.

crdtInfra.title: CRDT Infrastructure
crdtInfra.intro: These tables support the sync engine but are NOT synced themselves (local-only).

extensionTables.title: Extension Tables
extensionTables.intro: Extensions can create their own tables. These are automatically namespaced to prevent conflicts.

auto.title: Automatic CRDT Setup
auto.description: When an extension creates a table via migrations, CRDT columns (haex_hlc, haex_column_hlcs) and the dirty/delete triggers are added automatically. Extension tables sync just like core tables.

syncServer.title: Sync Server Schema
syncServer.intro: The sync server (haex-sync-server) keeps an encrypted, append-only change log per space and authenticates clients via DID-signed requests.

rls.title: Per-Space Authorization
rls.description: Every read/write is authorized against the calling DID's membership in the space. The server cannot decrypt the contents - only authorized members can.

triggers.title: SQLite Triggers
triggers.intro: CRDT tables get three triggers automatically: INSERT and UPDATE mark the table as dirty for the next push; BEFORE DELETE additionally logs the deleted row's PKs into haex_deleted_rows so the deletion can be replicated.

auto.title: Auto-Generated
auto.description: Triggers are created by the Rust backend when a table is set up for CRDT sync. You don't need to create them manually.
